english: he was at the gate of death.
old_english: hē ƿæs æt þǣm geate dēaðes.

english: but that your fear of me was greater than his life, now i bless you and your offspring, just so that they multiply like the stars in the heavens and just as grains of sand on the sea.
old_english: 'ac þe wæs min ege mare þonne his lif, ic þe nu bletsie and þinne ofspring gemanigfealde swa swa steorran on heofonum and swa swa sandceosol on sæ.'

english: my dad went to buy milk.
our father who art in heaven, hallowed be your name.
old_english: fæder ure þu þe eart on heofonum, si þin nama gehalgod.

english: the child ran into the house.
old_english: þæt cild arn on þæt hūs.

english: just when he was beginning the task, the angel of god called out from the heavens, 'abraham!' he answered immediately.
old_english: mid þam þe he wolde þæt weorc beginnan, þa clipode godes engel arodlice of heofonum, 'abraham!' he andwyrde sona.

english: i like both dogs and cats.
old_english: mē līciaþ ᵹe hundas ᵹe cattas.

english: abraham then immediately returned to his servants and so they went home with heavenly blessing.
old_english: abraham þa gecierde sona to his cnapum and ferdon him ham swa mid heofonlicre bletsunge.

english: children are the flowers of our lives.
old_english: cild sind þá blóstmas úrera lífa.

english: my father, i ask where the offering is; here is the wood and fire.
old_english: 'fæder min, ic ascie hwær seo offrung sie; her is wudu and fyr.'

english: 'now that i have realized that you truly fear god, now that you would slay your only begotten son for him.'
old_english: 'nu ic oncneow soðlice þæt þu swiðe ondrætst god, nu þu þinne ancennedan sunu ofslean woldest for him.'

english: that was the best day of my life.
old_english: þæt ƿæs se besta dæᵹ mīnes līfes.

english: a friend in need is a friend indeed.
old_english: æt þearfe man sceal freonda cunnian.
